ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pork steak entry; no specific butterfly-cut or lean-only variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LK covers grilled pig meat broadly; no butterfly-specific or lean-trimmed sub-code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for grilled lean pork butterfly steak; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: pork loin or shoulder, lean, fat-trimmed, butterflied

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of grilled pork butterfly steak is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one medium-sized butterflied pork steak as served in a Western-style meal.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Food Composition Database — Grilled pork butterfly steak, lean (Food Code F06090009)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F06090009 is sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All core per_100g values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary_fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) are taken from this approved primary source without modification. Saturated_fat_g is estimated at 0.9 g/100 g from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked lean pork loin (NDB No. 10219), which is an approved secondary source, consistent with the HPB total fat figure of 2.8 g/100 g; this estimation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (consulted; not applicable for zero-carbohydrate foods)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Grilled lean pork butterfly steak contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as confirmed by HPB Singapore Food Composition Database. The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted and confirms that glycemic index testing is not conducted for foods with negligible carbohydrate content, as there is no measurable postprandial glucose response. GI is set to null (not 0), gi_category is set to not_applicable, and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 by definition. This approach is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database methodology for protein- and fat-dominant foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (46 mg/100 g) and high potassium (450 mg/100 g) supporting a favourable sodium-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles.
hypertension.guidance: Grilled lean pork butterfly steak is suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt, soy sauce, or high-sodium marinades, as its intrinsic sodium is well below the 200 mg/100 g threshold and its potassium content actively supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content with very high protein (32.5 g/100 g), producing no postprandial glucose excursion.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled lean pork butterfly steak is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains no carbohydrate or sugar, exerts no glycaemic load, and its high protein content promotes satiety and slows gastric emptying; sweet glazes or marinades containing honey, hoisin, or sugar must be avoided to preserve this benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (2.8 g/100 g) and estimated low saturated fat (~0.9 g/100 g) with moderate dietary cholesterol (130 mg/100 g); lean preparation minimises atherogenic lipid exposure.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled lean pork butterfly steak, with all visible fat removed and prepared by dry-heat grilling without added oils, presents a low saturated fat profile suitable for cardiovascular disease management, though cholesterol-sensitive individuals should monitor portion size and overall weekly red meat intake in line with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contains arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid), which can amplify pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat including lean pork to no more than 1–2 servings per week, pairing it with anti-inflammatory foods such as oily fish, leafy greens, and olive oil to offset the pro-inflammatory potential of arachidonic acid.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled pork butterfly steak (lean) is a Western-style preparation of pork in which a boneless cut — typically from the loin or shoulder — is butterflied (sliced open and flattened to an even thickness) and then grilled over direct heat until fully cooked through, developing a lightly charred exterior while remaining juicy inside. All separable external and internal fat is removed prior to cooking, yielding an exceptionally lean product with only 2.8 g of total fat per 100 g. The result is a high-protein (32.5 g/100 g), low-fat, zero-carbohydrate food with 157 kcal per 100 g. The sodium content is very low at 46 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 450 mg/100 g, making it compatible with DASH dietary patterns for hypertension management. Because it contains no carbohydrate or sugar, it exerts no glycaemic load and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The lean fat profile and dry-heat cooking method also make it appropriate for cardiovascular health-conscious diets. Clinically, the food is best consumed without sweet marinades, high-sodium sauces, or added oils to preserve its favourable nutrient profile. It is commonly served as a main protein at breakfast or dinner in Western-style meal contexts.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id F06090009, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95), which is an approved primary regional source. The food is an exact match in the source database. Saturated fat is estimated at ~0.9 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for comparable lean grilled pork (NDB 10219), an approved secondary source, consistent with the HPB total fat figure of 2.8 g/100 g.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database — lean grilled pork contains no carbohydrate by composition. Sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero as no sugars are present in plain lean grilled pork muscle meat. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a pure animal muscle meat product with no plant-derived fibre. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0; University of Sydney GI Database consulted and confirms GI testing is not applicable for zero-carbohydrate foods. Saturated_fat_g of 0.9 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked lean pork loin (NDB 10219), an approved secondary source, consistent with the HPB total fat of 2.8 g/100 g; this is the only value not directly from HPB. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and extended micronutrient panel beyond those reported are not available in the HPB Food Composition Database entry for this specific food item (F06090009); USDA FoodData Central was consulted for the saturated fat estimate only. Halal status is not confirmed as pork is inherently non-halal; is_halal set to false. This is a Western-cuisine food; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese mandatory multilingual naming requirements apply under G6.
